title: Pan-Seared Canadian Halibut with Soy Glaze
description: A simple yet flavorful dish that showcases the freshness of Canadian halibut. The soy glaze adds a sweet and savory element to the delicate fish.
instructions: Season the halibut fillets with salt, pepper, and thyme.
Heat the sesame oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the halibut for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 8-10 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
While the halibut is baking, combine the soy sauce, maple syrup, rice vinegar, grated ginger, and brown sugar in a small saucepan.
Bring the glaze to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2-3 minutes, or until slightly thickened.
Remove the skillet from the oven and brush the soy glaze over the halibut.
Return the skillet to the oven and bake for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the glaze is caramelized.
Serve the halibut immediately, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
